content:
# INVESTMENT MEMO: NEXUS AEROSPACE SYSTEMS

## Executive Summary  
**Investment Opportunity:** 
Defense sUAS market growing at 7.6% CAGR to $22.8B by 2030
, with 
Replicator initiative fielding thousands of attritable autonomous systems by 2025
. Nexus Aerospace has the only NDAA-compliant autonomy stack integrating with existing C2 systems, built by ex-Anduril engineers who shipped the first production Altius system.  
**Investment Thesis:** DoD is shifting from $50M prime-built systems to $500K attritable drones. 
Joint Interagency Task Force 401 now serves as principal advisor on counter-sUAS matters
, creating unified procurement standards favoring interoperable solutions.  
**Deal Terms:** $20M Series A at $60M pre-money. Target 12% ownership with pro-rata rights.

## Market Opportunity  
**Defense sUAS TAM:** 
$16.9B by 2028, growing from $9.3B in 2020 at 7.9% CAGR
  
**Key Market Drivers:**  
• 
Replicator 2 focuses on counter-sUAS for critical installations and force concentrations
  
• 
FY26 NDAA expands counter-UAS authorities across DoD, DHS, DOE, and DOJ
  
• 
$500M in FY24 and FY25 Replicator funding
 driving attritable system procurement  
**Policy Tailwinds:** 
FY26 NDAA Section 842 directs DoD to strengthen U.S. small UAS industrial base
 while reducing foreign dependency

## Company Overview  
**Product:** AI-powered autonomy stack for sUAS enabling swarm coordination and C2 integration  
**Key Differentiators:**  
• Only solution with 
ITAR-compliant architecture handling CUI and controlled data securely
  
• 
Modular Open Systems Approach (MOSA) supporting future C2 integration
  
• Proven interoperability with 
existing command and control nodes via UHF/VHF, Satcom, 4G
  
**Traction:** $2.3M SBIR Phase II awarded; prototype validated with 82nd Airborne

## Investment Thesis  
**Core Insight:** 
DoD's second Replicator tranche focuses on software enabling system collaboration against dynamic threats
. Current solutions are hardware-centric; Nexus provides the missing software layer.  
**Team Match:** Co-founders led Anduril's Altius program through production. CTO holds DSP-5/DSP-73 clearances from Lockheed tenure, enabling rapid ITAR compliance.  
**Timing Catalyst:** 
Joint Interagency Task Force 401 must coordinate all DoD counter-sUAS efforts
, creating demand for unified autonomous command systems.

## Competitive Landscape  
**Direct:** Anduril (Lattice OS), L3Harris (ASView), General Atomics (Optix.C2)  
**Advantages:**  
• 
Anduril's $14B valuation proves startups can beat traditional primes with modern software
  
• Nexus's modular approach vs. competitors' proprietary ecosystems  
• Faster development cycles (18-month delivery vs. 36-month prime timelines)

## Key Risks & Mitigants  
**ITAR Compliance Burden:** Complex export controls limit sales velocity  
*Mitigant:* CTO's existing clearances and established DSP processes reduce compliance timeline by 8-12 months  
**Procurement Cycle Risk:** 
18-month government sales cycles
 strain cash flow  
*Mitigant:* SBIR Phase III direct-award pathway bypasses full competition  
**Technical Integration:** Legacy C2 systems resist new software integration  
*Mitigant:* 
Government evaluates interoperability with commercial and government C2 systems


## Deal Terms  
**Valuation:** $60M pre-money ($80M post)  
**Round Size:** $20M Series A  
**Ownership Target:** 12% fully-diluted with pro-rata rights  
**Use of Funds:**  
• Engineering/Autonomy R&D: 40% ($8M)  
• ITAR facility buildout: 25% ($5M)  
• Business development: 20% ($4M)  
• Operations/Working capital: 15% ($3M)

**Lead Investor:** Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) American Dynamism fund  
**Strategic Value:** Portfolio includes defense contractors with proven DoD relationships and ITAR experience
